notes_md:
**công an** = police (the police force or a police officer). It can mean the whole institution or an individual officer, depending on context.

It’s a noun. You can say **một công an** (one police officer), but people often add a classifier like **một người công an**. Common pairings: **đồn công an** (police station), **cảnh sát giao thông** (traffic police, a branch of công an), **lực lượng công an** (the police force).

To talk about a specific officer in a friendly way, people sometimes say **chú công an** (uncle police) when speaking to or about a male officer.
exampleSentences: Nếu em bị mất đồ, hãy báo với công an gần nhất.
Hôm qua, công an đến trường để nói chuyện về an toàn giao thông.
Chị thấy công an đang kiểm tra giấy tờ xe trên đường phố.
notes:
công an = police (the police force or a police officer). It can mean the whole institution or an individual officer, depending on context.

It’s a noun. You can say một công an (one police officer), but people often add a classifier like một người công an. Common pairings: đồn công an (police station), cảnh sát giao thông (traffic police, a branch of công an), lực lượng công an (the police force).

To talk about a specific officer in a friendly way, people sometimes say chú công an (uncle police) when speaking to or about a male officer.
exampleSentencesTranslated: If you lose something, report it to the nearest police.
Yesterday, police came to the school to talk about traffic safety.
I saw police checking vehicle documents on the street.
